title: EWMA covariance with calibrated lambda (drop RiskMetrics 0.94)
source: quant-phd-advisor re-review (2026-05-30) under the long-horizon + future-equity frame, verified against a reproduced full-history backtest (scripts/backtest_survival_book.py, 2000-01-03 -> 2026-05-29, 26.4y).
why: Current cov is equal-weighted sample (cov.cov()). An EWMA covariance reacts faster to intra-week crypto correlation regime shifts -- the exact risk of holding weights up to a week. EWMA ~= DCC on covariance-forecast error at far lower parameter burden (so DCC-GARCH is explicitly NOT pursued). RiskMetrics lambda=0.94 is a convention, not an optimum (Bollen 2015; ~0.97 better for monthly, asset-specific) -- calibrate lambda on the long backtest and report sensitivity. Pairs with the existing stress_covariance corr-floor (EWMA reacts fast; the floor is the worst-case backstop).
value_rationale: M: robustness, not detectable live-Sharpe; partially overlaps the existing corr-floor so net new protection is incremental.
scope: Replace equal-weighted cov.cov() (survival_book.py:804) with an EWMA covariance.
Do NOT free-fit lambda in-sample (overfitting hazard on the ~4y crypto window): use a literature default (0.94/0.97) OR choose via B0112's robust-plateau / DSR gate.
Layer order: EWMA estimate THEN stress_covariance corr-floor.
MEASURE the combined EWMA + corr-floor effect on crypto var-cap binding (two correlation-inflators can over-de-risk crypto beyond intent).
recommendation: Safe estimator change (no exposure increase). Use a literature lambda or gate the choice on B0112; never free-fit.

history.reason: risk-officer: no in-sample lambda fit; measure combined corr-inflation
